title: Garlicky Fluffy Japanese Beef Shin Soup
description: A comforting, low‑FODMAP Japanese soup featuring tender beef shin, aromatic garlic‑infused oil, and a silky, fluffy broth whisked with egg whites.
instructions: Heat a large pot over medium heat and add the garlic‑infused oil.
Sear the beef shin on all sides until browned, about 4-5 minutes per side.
Pour in the water, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a low simmer and cover. Cook for 3 hours until the meat is fall‑off‑the‑bone tender.
Remove the beef shin and strain the broth to remove solids. Return the clear broth to the pot.
Stir in tamari, mirin, rice wine, salt and white pepper, adjusting seasoning to taste.
In a small bowl, whisk the egg whites until frothy. Slowly drizzle the egg whites into the hot broth while stirring gently to create a light, airy foam.
Add the sliced spring onion and nori flakes, simmer for an additional 2 minutes until fragrant.
Serve the soup hot, garnishing with extra nori if desired.
